conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have one child, Alex, who's 17 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I have a few questions to help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Does Alex receive any education or approved training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education - studying for A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! 

Is Alex studying full-time (more than 12 hours a week of supervised study or course-related work experience)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's full-time - the usual sixth form schedule with classes throughout the week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. And do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions:

Are you the person responsible for Alex - meaning you're the one who is mainly looking after them and they live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex lives with me and I'm the one looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

Is Alex enrolled in approved education or training that is due to end before their 20th birthday? (This could be A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs, or similar approved courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, the A-levels will finish well before Alex turns 20 - they should be done next year actually.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Alex is 17 years old and studying full-time A-levels at sixth form college. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim for a child under 20 years old if they are in approved full-time education or training (more than 12 hours a week). Since Alex lives with you in the UK and you're responsible for their care, you meet all the eligibility requirements.

You can apply for Child Benefit if you haven't already done so. If you have any other questions about the process, feel free to ask!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and in full-time approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit for a child under 20 if they are in approved full-time education or training (more than 12 hours a week of supervised study). Alex meets this criteria as they are studying A-levels full-time, which will finish before they turn 20. The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives with them, and they are responsible for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old child Alex, who lives with them in the UK and is in full-time approved education (A-levels). Alex meets the age and education requirements, and the claimant is the responsible person caring for the child.
